originalTitle: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
title: A Big Lesson About Fairness in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, amazing children of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here, and today we have a very important story to share with you from a big country called Nigeria. It's about fairness, honesty, and why it's so important for everyone to do the right thing, especially people who help run our countries.

Meet Mr. Saleh Mamman

Once upon a time, there was a man named Mr. Saleh Mamman. He used to have a very important job in Nigeria. He was called a 'Minister.' Can you say 'Minister'? It's a big word for someone who helps the President of a country make important decisions and take care of things.

What is a Minister's Job?

Imagine your teacher helping you learn new things, or your parents making sure you have food and a home. A Minister is like that, but for a whole country! They are chosen to help with big things like schools, hospitals, roads, or even electricity. Did you know? A 'Minister' is like a special helper in the government, chosen to work on a big part of how a country runs!

Mr. Mamman Was a 'Power Minister'

Mr. Mamman's special job was being the 'Power Minister.' This means he was in charge of electricity! Think about it – the lights in your home, the fans that keep you cool, the TV you watch, and the machines that power hospitals and schools. All of these need electricity. So, Mr. Mamman's job was to make sure that people in Nigeria had electricity, and that the electricity system worked well for everyone. It was a very big and important responsibility!

What is 'Corruption'? (The Big Problem)

Now, here comes a tricky word: 'corruption.' It's like a bad secret. Imagine you have a special box of crayons that belongs to the whole class. Your teacher asks you to take care of it and make sure everyone gets to use the crayons to draw beautiful pictures. But then, you decide to hide some of the best crayons and use them only for yourself, or even sell them and keep the money! That's not fair, right? And the other children can't draw because they don't have enough crayons. 'Corruption' is when someone in a powerful job, like a Minister, uses their special position to take money or things that belong to everyone in the country. Instead of using that money to build schools or fix hospitals, they keep it for themselves or give it to their friends. This is wrong because it hurts all the people. Did you know? Public money is like the big piggy bank for the whole country, used to build good things for everyone, like roads and hospitals!

Why is Corruption Bad?

When powerful people are 'corrupt,' it means the money that should make life better for everyone disappears. Maybe a new school can't be built, or a hospital doesn't have enough medicine, or the electricity stops working because someone took the money meant to fix it. Corruption makes things unfair and stops a country from growing strong. It's like stealing from every single child, parent, and grandparent in the country. That's why it's a very serious problem.

The Judge's Decision: A 'Sentence'

Well, someone found out that Mr. Saleh Mamman did some 'corrupt' things. People called 'authorities' (like special police and judges) investigated and looked very carefully at what happened. They gather clues, just like detectives in a story! After looking at all the evidence, a special person called a 'judge' made a very important decision. A judge is like a referee in a game, making sure the rules are followed and things are fair. The judge decided that Mr. Mamman was 'guilty' of corruption. This means he broke the rules and did something very wrong with the country's money. Did you know? Judges wear special black robes to show how serious and important their job is!

75 Years – That's a Loooong Time!

Because what Mr. Mamman did was so serious, the judge gave him a 'sentence.' A 'sentence' means the judge decided what should happen to him because he broke the law. The judge decided that Mr. Mamman should go to jail for a very, very long time – 75 years! Imagine counting to 75. It's more years than your grandma or grandpa have lived! This long sentence shows everyone how serious corruption is and that people who do bad things with public money will be held accountable.

Where is He Now? (A Little Mystery)

Now, here's a little puzzle piece to our story. Even though the judge said Mr. Mamman should go to jail for 75 years, the people who work for the country, the 'authorities,' do not know where he is right now. They can't find him! It's like playing hide-and-seek, but in a very serious way. They are still looking for him so that he can face the consequences of his actions, just like the judge said. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa, with many, many people working hard to make it a great place!

Why This Matters to YOU!

This story might seem like it's just about grown-ups, but it's really important for you too! When people in power are honest and fair, it means there's more money to build better schools for you, to get more books, to have more medicines in hospitals, and to make sure your homes have electricity. It means your future Africa will be stronger, healthier, and fairer. You deserve a country where everyone plays by the rules and works for the good of all.

Fighting for a Fair Africa

Many wonderful people in Nigeria and all over Africa are working very hard to stop corruption. They want to make sure that money meant for the people goes to the people, not into someone's secret pocket. It's like a big team effort to keep our countries honest and strong. And you, children, are a very important part of this future! By learning about fairness and honesty now, you'll grow up to be amazing citizens who help build a better world. Thank you for listening, my little leaders of tomorrow! Always remember to be fair, be honest, and believe in a bright future for Africa!
summary: A former Nigerian Minister, Mr. Saleh Mamman, who was in charge of electricity, was found guilty of corruption by a judge and sentenced to a very long time in jail, but the authorities are still looking for him.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that taking money that belongs to everyone (corruption) is a very serious bad act, and people who do it will be punished so that our countries can be fair and grow strong for all children.

originalTitle: Could Iran war trigger a hunger crisis?
title: Could Iran war trigger a hunger crisis?
content: The UN warns disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could drive up food and fertiliser costs, and worsen global hunger.

originalTitle: ‘China is gaining from what the US is doing in Iran’
title: Big News from Far Away! Countries, Friends, and Trading!
content:
Hello, Amazing Explorers!

Hi everyone! It's your super-duper news reporter from Africa, ready to tell you about exciting things happening all around our big, beautiful world! Today, we're going on a quick trip to learn about some special friendships and happenings between countries that are far, far away. We're going to talk about some grown-up stuff, but don't worry, we'll make it super simple and fun, like a wonderful story! Are you ready for an adventure? Let's go!

Meet Our Friends: Countries!

Imagine our world is like a giant playground with many different groups of friends playing together. We call these groups of friends "countries"! Each country has its own special people, its own games, and its own special ways of doing things. Just like you might share your colourful toys with a friend, countries love to share things too! They buy and sell things to each other, and we call this important activity "trading." Trading helps everyone get what they need and want, like delicious food, cool toys, or even the fuel for our cars. It’s like a big swap meet where everyone gets something good!. Did you know? Our amazing continent, Africa, has lots and lots of countries, each with its own beautiful stories and people! Each one is so special.

What's Happening Far Away?

Now, sometimes, even the best friends on the playground don't always agree on everything. And sometimes, countries don't always agree either. Today, we're thinking about two big countries very far from us: America and Iran. America is a very big country far away, across the big blue ocean, and Iran is another country not too far from our home, but still quite a distance to travel! Right now, America and Iran are having some tough times. They are not agreeing on many things, and it's a bit like when you and a friend have a little disagreement. When countries don't agree, they sometimes put special rules on each other.

A Puzzle for Iran

Because America and Iran are having these tough times, America has put some special rules on Iran. These rules are mostly about buying and selling things, especially something super important called "oil." Oil is like a special energy drink for machines! It helps cars drive very fast, planes fly high in the sky, and it even helps make electricity so our lights turn on and our phones work. Iran has a lot of this special oil deep under its ground. But America's rules mean that it's much, much harder for Iran to sell its oil to lots of other countries. This is like having your favourite toys, but not many friends can play with them right now. It's a big puzzle and a bit of a challenge for Iran. Did you know? Oil comes from deep, deep under the ground! It's been there for millions of years, made from tiny ancient plants and animals!

Here Comes China!

But guess what? Another very big country, called China, is also on our world playground! China is super clever and always looking for ways to make new friends and trade. China needs a lot of oil for all its cars, factories, and to make all the cool things we use every day, like toys and phones. When America made it harder for Iran to sell its oil, China thought, "Hmm, maybe we can help!" So, China started buying lots and lots of oil from Iran. This turned out to be a very good deal for both China and Iran. Iran can still sell its oil, which it needs to do to help its people, and China gets all the oil it needs at a good price! It's like finding a new friend to play with your toys when others can't. A smart grown-up named Mr. Steve Okun, who watches what countries do, says that China is getting a really good opportunity from this. Did you know? China makes many of the cool toys, clothes, and electronic gadgets we use every day! They are very good at making things!

Why is Oil So Important?

Let's talk a little more about why oil is such a big deal. Imagine your dad's car, your school bus, or even a big truck carrying food to your town. How do they move? They need fuel to make their engines work! And most of that fuel comes from oil. So, every country needs oil to keep things running smoothly, to make lights turn on in our homes, and to help people travel from one place to another. That's why it's such a big deal when a country like Iran has lots of it, and a country like China needs lots of it. Oil is like a special energy juice for our modern world!

Making Friends and Sharing

This story about America, Iran, and China shows us how all countries are connected, just like a big family! What one country does can change things for another country, sometimes in small ways, and sometimes in big ways. It's like when one friend decides to share their yummy snack, and suddenly everyone gets a little bit happy! Or when a country makes a new rule, it can make other countries think about how they will buy and sell things. It really shows how our world is all linked together. Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in the world! That's a lot of different people and places to learn about!

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

* The United States of America is often just called the USA. It's a huge country with many famous cities and beautiful places! * Iran is a country in a part of the world called the Middle East. It has a very, very long history with ancient buildings and stories! * China is in Asia and has the most people of any country in the whole wide world! Wow, imagine so many people! * When countries trade things, they often send them on big, big ships across the vast oceans, or in huge airplanes high in the sky! * Being friendly and talking to each other is always the best way for countries to get along, just like it is for us with our friends and family! Kindness makes the world a better place.

What Does It All Mean for Us?

So, what does this story about countries far away mean for us here in Africa? Well, it teaches us that everyone is connected! When countries trade, or when they have disagreements, it can affect the prices of things we buy, like the fuel for cars or even some of the cool toys we play with. It shows us that it's good to learn about the world and how different countries work together, or sometimes, don't work together. Knowing these things helps us understand our big, amazing world a little better every day. It reminds us that we are all part of a global family.

Let's Be Kind and Share!

The most important thing to remember from our news adventure today is that even when countries have tough times, there are always ways to find solutions. And being kind, talking things through, and sharing are super important for everyone, everywhere in the world. Thank you for joining me on this news adventure! See you next time, super explorers! Keep learning and keep smiling!
summary: A smart grown-up says that China is getting good deals and opportunities because America and Iran are having a difficult time and not agreeing on many things, especially about selling oil.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how countries are all connected, and what happens far away can change how everyone buys and sells things, even for us here in Africa. It helps us learn about our big, amazing world and why it's good for countries to be friends!
